pattern: Because a rejected student simply rejoins the back of the line, only the COUNT of students still preferring each sandwich type matters, not their original order, so the whole simulation collapses to two running counters.
hints: A student who refuses the top sandwich just goes to the end of the queue and will be reconsidered later, so their exact position stops mattering the moment you realize the only thing that matters is how many students of each preference remain.
Track just two counts: how many remaining students want a circular sandwich and how many want a square one; the stack of sandwiches never changes order, only the students shuffle.

Walk the sandwich stack top to bottom: if any student left in the queue still wants the current top type, one of them takes it (decrement that count); the instant neither remaining preference count matches the current top, everyone left is stuck forever.

edge_inputs.description: single student matching the single sandwich
single student not matching the single sandwich
every student wants circular but every sandwich is square
everyone matches immediately, no one is ever stuck
stuck partway through: the front group eats but a leftover minority never gets served

stress.description: random preference and stack arrays up to the max constraint size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    students = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    sandwiches = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [students, sandwiches]
